name: 2,4,5-Trimethoxybenzaldehyde
description: 2,4,5-Trimethoxybenzaldehyde is an aromatic aldehyde reported in Acorus phytochemistry. Direct clinical evidence for efficacy or safety is sparse, so interpretation is largely mechanistic/contextual.
effects: Identified as an aromatic constituent in Acorus-related phytochemical profiles.
Direct human pharmacology evidence remains limited.
contraindications: Insufficient direct clinical safety evidence; avoid high-dose supplemental use.
interactions: No compound-specific interaction trials identified; uncertainty remains high.

sources.title: Alpha- and beta-asarone toxicology, interaction potential, and human safety context: a systematic review.
